description_long: This is a vintage military biplane with a distinctive twin-boom tail and a central fuselage, painted in a camouflage pattern of tan, green, and white with Royal Air Force roundels on the wings and fuselage. The aircraft features two large propellers mounted on the front of the lower wings, and the wings themselves are straight with no visible sweep or dihedral, connected by struts and wires. The tail section is an H-tail configuration, with two vertical stabilizers connected by a horizontal stabilizer. The cockpit is located at the front of the central fuselage and appears to be enclosed, likely accommodating a single pilot. The landing gear is fixed and consists of two main wheels under the wings and a small tail wheel at the rear. The overall design suggests a World War II-era reconnaissance or light attack aircraft.
description: A vintage military biplane with twin-boom H-tail, camouflage paint, dual front propellers, and fixed landing gear, marked with RAF roundels.
description_view_0: Side view showing the twin-boom tail, camouflage paint, and RAF roundels on the wings and fuselage.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the dual propellers, straight wings, and fixed main landing gear.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the twin-boom tail, cockpit placement, and detailed wing structure.
description_view_3: Rear view emphasizing the H-tail configuration, twin vertical stabilizers, and the alignment of the landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Straight mid-wing biplane configuration with no visible dihedral or winglets; wings are braced with struts and wires.
attribute_engine_type: twin piston engines mounted on the front of the lower wings with two-bladed propellers
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers connected by a horizontal stabilizer
attribute_landing_gear_type: fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a single tail wheel
attribute_cockpit_placement: forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit at the front of the central fuselage
